post.headline: Add your encryption key during setup
post.subtext: Add a public PGP key during onboarding so your tip line is ready for encrypted submissions before you share it.
post.image_alt_text: Social graphic with a mobile Hush Line onboarding screen on a light background. The image centers step 2 of setup, where a new account holder adds a public PGP key using Proton Mail search or manual paste before continuing.

social.bluesky: Setting up Hush Line? Add your public PGP key during onboarding before you share your tip line. It makes encrypted submissions available from the start, so sources can use the secure path right away.
social.linkedin:
If you are setting up Hush Line for a newsroom, law office, school, or other trusted inbox, add your public PGP key before you share your tip line.

This step in onboarding makes encrypted submissions available from the start, so sources do not reach a profile that cannot receive secure tips yet.
social.mastodon: Setting up Hush Line? Add your public PGP key during onboarding before you share your tip line. That makes encrypted submissions available from the start, so sources do not reach a profile that is not ready for secure tips yet.

post.rationale: Chosen from the latest screenshot release v0.6.0 captured on 2026-03-19 because it clearly shows recent shipped onboarding work from PR #1743 and a core documented recipient need: adding a public PGP key so sources can send encrypted disclosures. It is an above-the-fold light-mode mobile screen, avoids empty states, uses the latest release, and does not repeat any archived concept.
post.copy_brief: Write for recipients and staff using Hush Line day to day, not for platform admins.

matched_pull_requests.title: #1733 Phase 6: modernize auth and onboarding core form flows

post.title: Onboarding - Step 2 Encryption (newman)

summary: Use the onboarding encryption screen to show that a new Hush Line account should add a public PGP key before sharing a tip line.
